meta.disclaimer: Deadlines and eligibility change yearly. Always verify on the official URL before applying. Database curated for multiple explorers (Merey at HKU BME; Bob at HKU Global Health/Gov&Laws; Maja at Aalto ChemE). Use profile chips to filter. Not affiliated with HKU or Aalto. Hackathons tab lists make-a-thons curated for product design + entrepreneurship practice; dates shift yearly—verify official URLs.

opportunities.name: ETH Student Summer Research Fellowship
opportunities.what_you_get: Summer research fellowship (often CS/computational focus)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Competitive; check current fields open to BME/computational

opportunities.notes: Real tourism ambassadors are established influencers. Legitimate programmes never charge fees or ask for crypto/gift cards.
opportunities.creative_angle: Ignore. Build real competition/research wins instead.
opportunities.name: Tourism Board / Airline 'Student Ambassador' DM Offers
opportunities.what_you_get: Usually nothing — or identity theft
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Anyone who falls for DMs

opportunities.name: French + Sciences (Consulate General of France in HK/Macau)
opportunities.funder: Consulate General of France in Hong Kong and Macau / Campus France
opportunities.what_you_get: 3–4 week STEM+French immersion in France; scholarship often covers programme fee, round-trip flights, small living allowance, insurance
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG science/technology students at a HK or Macau university; beginner French OK; English-speaking; competitive record + motivation
opportunities.notes: Also see campusfrance.org French + Sciences. Small scholarship count.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pitch BME via micro-nanotech or AI tracks.

opportunities.name: EMBL Lautenschläger Summer School (Visualising Life)
opportunities.what_you_get: 2-week imaging/ML biology school at EMBL Heidelberg; €200 fee covers housing+meals; travel grants and fee waivers available
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Advanced Bachelor/Master in Physics, Chemistry, Engineering, Math, CS (non-biology backgrounds targeted); ~20 places
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Often early April for July school
opportunities.notes: Ask for travel grant + fee waiver in the same application.
opportunities.creative_angle: Ideal BME→bioimaging bridge.

opportunities.name: EMBL TechDev Internship Programme
opportunities.what_you_get: Hands-on technology/tool-development internship; funding negotiated with host (not automatic)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Bachelor/Master students in engineering/physics/math/CS interested in instrumentation
opportunities.notes: Ask explicitly about housing/stipend before accepting.
opportunities.creative_angle: Perfect for BME device/optics/hardware; combine with HKU travel funds.

opportunities.name: NIMS Internship Program (Japan)
opportunities.what_you_get: Up to 90-day materials-science research internship; daily allowance + accommodation fees; airfare usually self-funded
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Current UG or graduate students worldwide; contact host researcher via SAMURAI directory first; host submits application
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Next round often ~Dec — confirm on NIMS page
opportunities.notes: Third-party blogs claiming full airfare are often inaccurate.
opportunities.creative_angle: Target implant/biomaterial groups; stack HKU travel subsidy for flights.

opportunities.name: JST Sakura Science Exchange Program
opportunities.what_you_get: Short science exchange in Japan; JST covers airfare, in-Japan transport/accommodation/meals via Japanese host org
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG/grad ≤40; typically first Japan visit after entering university; cannot apply solo — Japanese host must invite you
opportunities.notes: Ask a Japanese collaborator of your HKU supervisor to include you.
opportunities.creative_angle: Medical-theme Sakura exchanges exist when Japanese medical schools host.

opportunities.name: ISMRM Educational Stipend (Annual Meeting)
opportunities.funder: International Society for Magnetic Resonance in Medicine
opportunities.what_you_get: Partial travel/registration support to present at ISMRM Annual Meeting; max 3 years of stipend support
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Full-time UG/grad students; ISMRM membership + dues by stipend deadline; abstract required; first authors prioritized
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Membership/stipend often late Oct — check current year
opportunities.notes: Do not register until stipend results arrive. Stack with Engg Innovation Academy.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pair with HKU Engg travel subsidy to close funding gap.

opportunities.name: ISMRM E.K. Zavoisky Stipend
opportunities.what_you_get: Meeting attendance support for scientists from financially restricted countries with limited personal income
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Must reside in a financially restricted country AND have limited personal income; first-author abstracts prioritized
opportunities.notes: Eligibility hinges on residence, not citizenship. Living in HK may disqualify — confirm with ISMRM.
opportunities.creative_angle: Only pursue if ISMRM confirms HK residence is acceptable.

opportunities.name: MICCAI Society Registration & Travel Grants
opportunities.what_you_get: Registration grants (free registration + membership) and travel grants up to ~USD 2,500; RISE prioritizes LMIC/underrepresented
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Accepted paper usually required for travel grant; priority to students/young scientists and underrepresented/LMIC applicants
opportunities.year_of_study: UG rare via workshop; typically MSc/PhD
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Travel grant apps often ~1 Jul — verify each year
opportunities.notes: Kazakhstan nationality may help RISE framing; HK affiliation is not LMIC.
opportunities.creative_angle: Join HKU medical-imaging/AI lab; target workshop paper + RISE if eligible.

opportunities.name: SPIE Student Conference Support (Medical Imaging)
opportunities.what_you_get: Up to ~USD 700 domestic / ~USD 1,000 international airfare/lodging reimbursement and/or registration waiver
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Active SPIE Student Member; presenting author of accepted paper; UG/grad; one award every two years
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Check SPIE Medical Imaging attend page (event often Feb)
opportunities.notes: Also useful for Photonics West if imaging-optics overlap.
opportunities.creative_angle: Cheap SPIE student membership + accepted paper is the gate.

opportunities.name: ASME Summer Bioengineering Conference Travel Award + Student Paper Competition
opportunities.what_you_get: Free in-person registration + mentoring for Culture & Community Travel Award; Student Paper Competition cash prizes at BS/MS/PhD levels
opportunities.eligibility_summary: STEM UG/grad with accepted SBC abstract; Culture award prioritizes first-time attendees who could not otherwise attend; SPC has BS track
opportunities.apply_window: Abstract winter/spring; travel award after acceptance
opportunities.notes: Stronger BME conference funding path than BMES Annual for many internationals.
opportunities.creative_angle: Submit to BS Student Paper Competition + Culture travel award + HKU Engg stack.

opportunities.name: TERMIS-EU ID&E Conference Scholarship
opportunities.what_you_get: Full TERMIS-EU registration fee reimbursement after conference
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Conditions emphasize Master/PhD with accepted abstract as presenting author; long-distance travel OR country HDI <0.850
opportunities.notes: Treat UG as closed unless confirmed. Strong later for tissue-eng MSc.
opportunities.creative_angle: Kazakhstan HDI may help at postgrad stage.

opportunities.name: HKU Faculty of Engineering Innovation Academy Travel Subsidy
opportunities.funder: HKU Faculty of Engineering Innovation Academy
opportunities.what_you_get: Up to ~70% of travel+accommodation for Faculty/InnoWing-endorsed overseas experiential learning
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Engineering UG/TPg and InnoHub members; activity must be endorsed; competitive quotations and expense rules apply
opportunities.notes: Use as co-funding layer under every external conference stipend.
opportunities.creative_angle: Stack under ISMRM/SPIE/MICCAI/ASME awards.

opportunities.name: HKU iDendron / TEC Competition Subsidies
opportunities.what_you_get: Subsidies to join renowned international innovation/entrepreneurship competitions outside Hong Kong
opportunities.eligibility_summary: HKU students entering endorsed external entrepreneurship competitions
opportunities.notes: Pair with James Dyson / ClimateLaunchpad / Falling Walls pipelines.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use for medtech startup competition travel.

opportunities.name: Falling Walls Lab
opportunities.funder: Falling Walls Foundation + local Lab organisers
opportunities.what_you_get: Local Lab free to enter; winner gets trip to Berlin Global Finale (travel usually covered by local organiser) + Science Summit access
opportunities.eligibility_summary: University students and early/mid-career innovators; choose nearest Lab
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Apply to nearest Asia Lab when listed
opportunities.notes: Travel to Berlin funded only if you win a Lab.
opportunities.creative_angle: 3-minute medtech/BME breakthrough pitch.
opportunities.why_great: 3-minute breakthrough pitch format trains crisp product/science storytelling on a world stage.

opportunities.name: James Dyson Award
opportunities.what_you_get: National/international prize money for problem-solving design/engineering inventions (not a travel grant)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG or graduates within 4 years in engineering/product design; studied in eligible country including Hong Kong SAR
opportunities.notes: Prize money ≠ travel funding, but builds funded-travel narrative later.
opportunities.creative_angle: Enter a BME device/assistive-tech prototype from HKU labs.
opportunities.why_great: Design-engineering award that rewards physical product invention—ideal for BME/design builders.

opportunities.name: ClimateLaunchpad
opportunities.what_you_get: Free training bootcamp; national winners often receive travel to Global Grand Final
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Climate/green business ideas; early-stage; check HK and/or Kazakhstan national pages
opportunities.notes: Travel only if you win/qualify to Global Final.
opportunities.creative_angle: Health × climate innovation for Central Asia heat/air-quality.
opportunities.why_great: Climate venture competition with training bootcamps—entrepreneurship curriculum built in.

opportunities.name: Youth4Climate Call for Solutions (UNDP / Italy)
opportunities.funder: UNDP Rome Centre / Government of Italy
opportunities.what_you_get: Up to USD 30,000 seed funding + mentorship for youth-led climate projects
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Youth ~18–29 from UNDP-covered countries (Kazakhstan typically included; confirm annual list)
opportunities.notes: Seed funding may enable travel for implementation/events.
opportunities.creative_angle: BME + climate-health device for Central Asia.

opportunities.name: CSC China Link Short-term Scientific Exchange Scholarship
opportunities.what_you_get: 1–12 months research/study in China: tuition waiver, housing, insurance, stipend CNY 3,000/month; airfare usually not included
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Non-Chinese citizen; UG/Master as general scholar; must be enrolled at a CSC foreign partner university AND obtain Chinese host admission
opportunities.apply_window: Rolling monthly via Chinese host + CSC portal
opportunities.notes: VERIFY whether HKU appears on current CSC Foreign Partner University List.
opportunities.creative_angle: Kazakhstan + Belt-and-Road + Mandarin learning is a strong pitch if eligible.

opportunities.name: Chinese Government Scholarship International Summer School Programs
opportunities.what_you_get: 2 weeks–3 months summer schools: tuition, housing, insurance, stipend (~CNY 3,000/mo for UG/Master)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Foreign students enrolled overseas (not already studying in China); each host sets theme/eligibility
opportunities.notes: Not a single portal — track universities’ international colleges (SJTU, ZJU, SUSTech, etc.).
opportunities.creative_angle: Search biomedical/AI summer schools once CSC-funded lists post.

opportunities.name: TÜRKSOY / Turkic World Youth Forum
opportunities.funder: TÜRKSOY / Organization of Turkic States networks
opportunities.what_you_get: Invitation-based cultural/scientific youth forums; hosts often cover local costs; international travel coverage varies
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Youth from Turkic world countries (Kazakhstan citizenship is a strong fit); selection via national partners/embassies
opportunities.notes: Verify each invitation letter for travel coverage.
opportunities.creative_angle: Diaspora Kazakh student in HK bridging STEM and Turkic youth diplomacy.

opportunities.name: JENESYS (Japan-East Asia Network of Exchange for Students and Youths)
opportunities.what_you_get: ~10-day fully funded thematic visit to Japan (airfare, lodging, programme)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Country-specific invitations; Central Asia participation varies by year; apply via Japanese Embassy/local partner
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Check Embassy of Japan in Kazakhstan announcements
opportunities.notes: Living in HK may require applying via Kazakhstan pathway.
opportunities.creative_angle: Watch science/innovation themes; combine with later Sakura/NIMS stays.

opportunities.name: Tohoku University Engineering Summer Program (TESP)
opportunities.funder: Tohoku University / JASSO (for selected partner students)
opportunities.what_you_get: ~16-day English engineering programme (incl. bio-materials tracks); JASSO ~JPY 80,000 for selected partner students
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Only designated TESP partner universities; eligibility varies by course
opportunities.notes: VERIFY HKU partnership with Tohoku SoE.
opportunities.creative_angle: If HKU is a TESP partner, bio-materials track is on-profile.

opportunities.name: HKUST LIFS Summer Research Program
opportunities.what_you_get: ~HK$10,000 subsidy for hostel/local expenses; ~1-month research with LIFS faculty; student pays fees + flights
opportunities.eligibility_summary: End-of-Year-3 UG in Life Science/Chemistry/Biotech/Bioengineering etc. studying in Mainland or overseas; GPA ≥3.2/4.0
opportunities.notes: VERIFY: wording says Mainland/overseas — local HK universities may be excluded.
opportunities.creative_angle: If eligible as overseas relative to HKUST, strong BME→life-science trial.

opportunities.name: Institut Pasteur Amgen Scholars / Pasteur Foundation Summer
opportunities.what_you_get: Funded summer research in Paris (stipend/housing depending on track)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Pasteur Amgen: undergraduates enrolled in Bologna Process European universities. US track: US citizens only
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Ineligible for HKU/Kazakh profile under current tracks
opportunities.notes: CLOSED to HKU/Kazakh profile under current tracks.
opportunities.creative_angle: Revisit only if enrolled at an eligible European partner later.

opportunities.name: Francis Crick Institute Summer Student Training Programme
opportunities.what_you_get: Paid ~9-week summer research in London
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Must study at a UK university and meet widening-participation criteria; non-UK university students explicitly ineligible
opportunities.notes: Explicitly closed to non-UK university students.
opportunities.creative_angle: None currently.

opportunities.name: HHMI Janelia Undergraduate Scholars
opportunities.what_you_get: Historically fully supported ~10-week summer research (international UG/Master eligible when running)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Well-prepared UG/Master with significant independent research; must remain enrolled
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Programme paused in recent summers — monitor janelia.org
opportunities.notes: Speculative until a new cycle is announced.
opportunities.creative_angle: If reopened, strong fit for computational/imaging BME.

opportunities.name: Orkhun (Orhun) Exchange Programme – TURKUNIB
opportunities.what_you_get: 1–2 semester exchange among TURKUNIB universities; scholarship/travel terms set by protocols
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Must be enrolled at a TURKUNIB member university; HKU is not a typical member
opportunities.notes: Closed via HKU unless enrolled at a member uni.
opportunities.creative_angle: More relevant if you do a dual pathway at a Kazakh partner later.

opportunities.name: CMBE Postgraduate Travel Grants
opportunities.what_you_get: ~USD 500 registration reimbursement; separate best doctoral thesis award with waiver + travel
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Enrolled Master's/PhD with accepted CMBE abstract
opportunities.notes: Grad-only — later for computational BME thesis work.
opportunities.creative_angle: Keep for MPhil/PhD stage.

opportunities.name: BMES CMBE Graduate Student Travel Award
opportunities.what_you_get: Travel award + lightning talk at BMES CMBE conference
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Graduate students only (PhD not yet awarded) at submission
opportunities.notes: BMES Annual Meeting itself rarely offers universal student travel grants.
opportunities.creative_angle: Post-graduation / MPhil path.

opportunities.name: FameLab
opportunities.what_you_get: Science communication competition; some national programmes fund training travel; International winner gets Cheltenham experience
opportunities.eligibility_summary: STEM communicators; national eligibility varies; check whether HK/China/Kazakhstan runs a heat
opportunities.notes: Asia heats are intermittent.
opportunities.creative_angle: Build scicomm skills useful for Falling Walls too.
opportunities.why_great: Science communication competition that sharpens investor/user-facing explanations of technical products.

opportunities.name: HKU Faculty of Social Sciences SIGC Non-local Internships
opportunities.funder: HKU Faculty of Social Sciences (with possible Horizons/CEDARS top-ups)
opportunities.what_you_get: Credit-bearing summer placement (FOSS2018/FOSS2019); FOSS students may receive Faculty scholarships/subsidies; non-FOSS students generally unpaid and ineligible for FOSS scholarships—stack with Reaching Out Award / Horizons / EDB subsidies if eligible
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Priority to full-time FOSS majors/minors (incl. BSocSc Gov&Laws); Year 2+ recommended; non-FOSS non-final-year UGs may apply with 12 free-elective credits and ~2.5-month summer commitment; incoming exchange students ineligible
opportunities.language_req: English; host-country language may help for some posts
opportunities.apply_window: Typically early February via FOSS Experiential Learning system (e.g. 5–17 Feb 2025 for Summer 2025)
opportunities.notes: Gov&Laws curricula allow SI or GC fulfilment via these pathways—ideal curricular fit for Bob. Verify annual partner list and FOSS funding separately from placement offer.
opportunities.creative_angle: Prioritise think-tank, WHO-adjacent NGO, or health-policy placements in ASEAN/UK and combine with Reaching Out Award (once-only HK$10,000).

opportunities.name: UNICEF Internship Programme
opportunities.what_you_get: 6–26 weeks full- or part-time internship; monthly stipend toward living costs; possible one-time lump sum toward travel/visa when funding available (not guaranteed)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Students and recent graduates from diverse fields; specific vacancy criteria vary by office; open globally via UNICEF careers
opportunities.notes: Public Partnerships / health / child rights posts fit Bob. Stipend rarely covers full international relocation—pair with ROA/Horizons.
opportunities.creative_angle: Watch Asia-Pacific partnership/advocacy posts (e.g. Tokyo PPR) that blend health diplomacy and government relations.

opportunities.name: United Nations Secretariat Internship Programme
opportunities.what_you_get: 2–6 month unpaid internship; no UN remuneration; G-4 visa facilitation for US duty stations; high prestige for policy/law/governance CVs
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Enrolled in or recently graduated (within 1 year) from bachelor’s (final year), master’s, or PhD; strong English or French; not child/sibling of UN Secretariat staff
opportunities.year_of_study: Final-year bachelor’s, graduate, or within 1 year of graduation
opportunities.notes: Secretariat internships are unpaid—explicitly budget travel via HKU awards. Agency policies (WHO/UNICEF) differ and may pay stipends.
opportunities.creative_angle: Target OHCHR, DESA, or peacebuilding posts; use as prestige step before Chevening/Yenching later.

opportunities.name: HKU Worldwide / Faculty Exchange – Scholarship for Semester Exchange
opportunities.what_you_get: Merit scholarship automatically considered if CGPA ≥3.0 before exchange: Zone 1 (UK/US/Europe/etc.) HK$14,000/semester or HK$21,000/year; lower zones HK$4,000–13,000; notional top-up not full cost coverage; one largest enrichment award rule applies
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Full-time HKU undergraduates on approved university- or faculty-level exchange; CGPA ≥3.0 before exchange semester; open to local and non-local students
opportunities.gpa_hint: CGPA 3.0+ for scholarship; partner GPA may be higher
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Check IAO each semester; scholarship disbursement follows offer acceptance
opportunities.notes: Stack carefully: government SSE/SSEBR are LOCAL-only; ROA is once-only and usually cannot concurrent with other enrichment awards.
opportunities.creative_angle: Choose a partner with strong public-health/policy faculties (e.g. UK/Canada) so exchange doubles as field for thesis/Laidlaw follow-on.

opportunities.name: Subsidy Scheme for Student Learning Activities in the GBA
opportunities.what_you_get: HK$6,000 subsidy for eligible outbound GBA learning activities
opportunities.eligibility_summary: RESIDENCY TRAP: local undergraduate students encouraged/targeted; confirm current China Vision eligibility each year. For GBA interflow, GHMUA scheme reimburses cross-border transport up to HK$500.
opportunities.apply_window: Tied to China Vision activity calendar
opportunities.notes: Non-means-tested Mainland Experience Scheme is separately open to local and non-local students (once-only) for Mainland exchanges—better path for non-locals.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pair GBA subsidy with FOSS Global Bay Areas Impact Lab for policy/social-innovation fieldwork.

opportunities.name: FOSS Global Bay Areas Impact Lab (GBA / Tokyo Bay / SF Bay)
opportunities.funder: HKU Faculty of Social Sciences SIGC
opportunities.what_you_get: ~8-week SIGC experiential programme: orientation, ~5-week GBA placements, Tokyo Bay field trip (Waseda), virtual SF Bay seminars, hackathon; may fulfil SI/GC depending on year’s offer; FOSS funding rules apply
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Primarily FOSS undergraduates via SIGC application; check annual Internship Week listing
opportunities.apply_window: Via FOSS SIGC summer application (typically Feb)
opportunities.notes: Policy/social-innovation focus across three bay economies—strong for governments & laws + urban health angles.
opportunities.creative_angle: Position projects around cross-border health governance or GBA social-policy delivery for dual global-health/gov-law branding.

opportunities.name: HKU BASc(Global Health and Development) 6-month Field Placement
opportunities.funder: HKU School of Public Health / HKUMed
opportunities.what_you_get: Compulsory 6-month placement with UN agencies, NGOs, foundations, CROs etc.; limited HKU scholarships to support students during placement; mentoring and workshops
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Only students enrolled in BASc(GHD) programme; not an open university-wide call
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: N/A (programme component); scholarships announced within cohort
opportunities.notes: If Bob is (or transfers into) BASc(GHD), this is the flagship funded global-health path. Otherwise use SIGC/WHO/HYAB alternatives.
opportunities.creative_angle: Seek UN health / health-systems placements and document for later LSHTM/Chevening applications.

opportunities.name: Hague Academy of International Law – Summer Courses Scholarships
opportunities.funder: Hague Academy of International Law (public/private sponsors)
opportunities.what_you_get: Registration fees covered; travel reimbursement up to regional cap (Far East €800); €600 accommodation assistance at start; ~15% of attendees receive scholarships; Public IL session typically July
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Scholarship applicants must already hold an undergraduate law degree (BA/AB/LL.B. or equivalent) at time of application; master’s preferred. Course attendance also allows advanced non-LL.B. paths, but scholarship bar is higher.
opportunities.gpa_hint: Strong academic record; professor of international law recommendation required
opportunities.year_of_study: After LL.B./law degree; preferably master’s or early practitioner
opportunities.apply_window: Registration opens ~1 Nov annually; scholarships results ~April; 2026 on-site registration closed as of mid-2026
opportunities.notes: Ideal for Gov&Laws & LLB students after first law degree. Online courses exist but have NO scholarships.
opportunities.creative_angle: Apply scholarship only to Public International Law session; align essay with health-security/IHL or treaty-law interests.

opportunities.name: Philip C. Jessup International Law Moot – HKU Team (HK Rounds → Washington DC)
opportunities.funder: HKU Faculty of Law International Mooting Programme / ILSA / sponsors (e.g. White & Case)
opportunities.what_you_get: Team selection, coaching, and Faculty/sponsor support toward regional and (if champions) White & Case International Rounds in Washington DC; travel support typically via Faculty mooting funds/sponsors—confirm annually; prize prestige rather than cash
opportunities.eligibility_summary: HKU law students (LLB/PCLL/related) selected via Faculty internal mooting processes; open to UG and some PG tracks depending on year
opportunities.year_of_study: Law undergraduates and PCLL/advanced law students
opportunities.apply_window: Internal selection usually Semester 1; HK rounds ~Feb–Mar; International rounds ~Mar–Apr
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Watch HKU Law mooting page each autumn
opportunities.notes: HKU has repeatedly won HK Overall Champion and advanced to DC. Not a cash fellowship—real travel funding exists via Faculty but amounts vary.
opportunities.creative_angle: If dual Gov&Laws/LLB, treat Jessup as the flagship advocacy credential; stack ROA for DC if Faculty budget is tight.

opportunities.name: Chevening Scholarship (Hong Kong)
opportunities.what_you_get: Full UK master’s funding: tuition, monthly stipend, return airfare, arrival/departure allowances, visa cost, Chevening event travel grant
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Citizen of Chevening-eligible territory (HK eligible); bachelor’s completed ≥2 years before deadline; ≥2 years work experience; return to HK ≥2 years after award. BN(O) holders applying from HK have special dual-British citizenship exception. Not for those resident in UK at application.
opportunities.gpa_hint: Strong enough for competitive UK master’s offers
opportunities.year_of_study: Post-bachelor’s with work experience (not for current undergrads without experience)
opportunities.apply_window: Typically Aug–Nov annually; 2026–27 applications closed as of 2026
opportunities.notes: RESIDENCY/CITIZENSHIP: apply under the country/territory of citizenship. International students at HKU must check whether they apply via home country Chevening, not automatically via HK.
opportunities.creative_angle: Build 2+ years health-policy/NGO/government experience after graduation, then target LSHTM or Oxford/Cambridge public policy master’s.

opportunities.name: Fulbright Programme (Hong Kong / China exchanges)
opportunities.funder: U.S. Department of State (historically via Hong Kong-America Center)
opportunities.what_you_get: Historically funded study/research in the US; currently not operating for HK/China under US policy
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Fulbright exchanges for Hong Kong and mainland China were terminated/suspended by the US in 2020; US Student Program lists grants not available to Hong Kong. Do not plan on this pathway unless policy reverses.
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Monitor US Fulbright / State Department for any restart; no reliable near-term cycle
opportunities.notes: Included for clarity: closed_to_you under current policy. Prefer Chevening, Yenching, Schwarzman, LKYSPP, DAAD, MEXT instead.
opportunities.creative_angle: If US study is essential, pursue university merit aid or other country Fulbright only if Bob has another eligible citizenship.

opportunities.name: DAAD University Summer Courses in Germany for Foreign Students and Graduates
opportunities.what_you_get: Scholarship for ~4-week German language & area-studies / technical-language courses (tuition + DAAD stipend components per call); once-only programme funding
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Undergraduates (must have completed ≥2 years by course start) and master’s students enrolled in a programme country including Hong Kong (CN); age ≥18; German language skills at time of application sufficient to follow courses; enrolled throughout scholarship
opportunities.language_req: German (minimum level per course list; not English-taught)
opportunities.apply_window: Annual DAAD call (often autumn/winter for following summer); next call noted around Sep 2026 on DAAD pages
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Check DAAD scholarship database from Sep 2026 for Summer 2027
opportunities.notes: RISE Germany is NOT open to HK universities (North America/UK/Ireland only). This HSK summer course route is the realistic DAAD short-term option. Social-science/policy value is mainly language + EU regional studies.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use to unlock later German-taught policy/public-health master’s and DAAD longer research grants.

opportunities.name: Japanese Government (MEXT) Research Student Scholarship – Embassy Recommendation (Hong Kong)
opportunities.funder: MEXT / Consulate-General of Japan in Hong Kong
opportunities.what_you_get: 18–24 months (incl. possible 6 months language training): entrance fees, full tuition, monthly allowance, round-trip Hong Kong–Japan flight
opportunities.eligibility_summary: RESIDENCY TRAP: Consulate route targets Hong Kong/Macau SAR passport holders; age under 35 (born on/after date in annual guidelines); bachelor’s or final-year; study in same/related field. Non-HK passport holders must apply via Japanese mission of their nationality.
opportunities.language_req: English and/or Japanese (written tests + interview in Hong Kong)
opportunities.year_of_study: Final-year bachelor’s or graduate for research/master’s track
opportunities.apply_window: Typically Apr–Jun at Consulate (2026 intake closed; 2027 cycle expected Apr–May 2026)
opportunities.notes: Health policy / public policy / international law research plans are eligible if matched to Japanese graduate supervisors. Attend HK written test in person—no exceptions.
opportunities.creative_angle: Propose research on Japan’s universal health coverage / ASEAN health cooperation as a bridge to global-health diplomacy.

opportunities.name: LSHTM Fund Scholarship (London-based MSc)
opportunities.funder: London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine Fund
opportunities.what_you_get: One award typically covering overseas tuition (incl. mandatory field-trip fees) + tax-free stipend (~GBP 22,000 for 2026–27)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Offer-holders for eligible 1-year full-time London intensive MSc (excludes some joint programmes); first/upper-second UK equivalent bachelor’s; demonstrated financial need for this Fund Scholarship
opportunities.apply_window: Admissions + scholarship dual process; 2026–27 Fund Scholarship deadline was 4 Mar 2026
opportunities.notes: Short courses generally lack these major scholarships—target the full MSc. GSK Future Health Leaders awards are Africa-focused (likely closed_to_you).
opportunities.creative_angle: Apply to MSc Public Health or Health Policy-related intensive programmes; use WHO/FOSS experience in personal statement.

opportunities.name: Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health – Summer Institutes
opportunities.funder: Johns Hopkins Bloomberg SPH (limited institute scholarships)
opportunities.what_you_get: Intensive short courses (e.g. Global Health Systems; Health Policy and Management; Gender and Health). Occasional registration-fee / general scholarships—tuition otherwise self-funded; not a travel fellowship
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Non-degree seekers typically need bachelor’s equivalent and readiness for graduate-level work; for-credit requires JHU enrolment
opportunities.apply_window: Course registration + separate scholarship forms (often spring)
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Verify each institute page each spring (scholarships historically ~Apr)
opportunities.notes: Treat as skills upgrade, not funded travel. Combine online options with HKU funding if physical Baltimore attendance is needed.
opportunities.creative_angle: Take Global Health Systems / Health Policy institutes to signal readiness for WHO or LSHTM applications.

opportunities.name: Lee Kuan Yew School of Public Policy (NUS) – Merit Graduate Scholarships
opportunities.funder: LKY School of Public Policy / NUS
opportunities.what_you_get: Merit scholarships for MPP/MIA/MPA typically covering tuition, monthly maintenance allowance, book/settling-in allowances, health insurance, and travel to/from home country (exact package varies by award); ~half of international students receive some aid
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Strong academics, leadership, public-service commitment; indicate scholarship interest on online application; fresh graduates welcome for MPP; work experience valued especially for MPA
opportunities.apply_window: Typically Aug–15 Dec for MPP/MIA; MPA often to 31 Dec
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: 15 Dec annually for main MPP/MIA scholarship consideration
opportunities.notes: Asia’s leading public-policy school; strong fit for governments & laws with health-policy electives. No bond on many school scholarships.
opportunities.creative_angle: Frame application around ASEAN health diplomacy / GBA–Singapore policy learning; pursue double-degree options later if relevant.

opportunities.name: Rotary Peace Fellowship (Master’s / Professional Certificate)
opportunities.what_you_get: Up to ~50 master’s and ~120 certificate fellowships yearly covering tuition/fees, room and board, round-trip transport, internship/field-study expenses
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Master’s track: bachelor’s + ≥3 years relevant full-time work/volunteer; certificate: ≥5 years. English proficiency; leadership and peace/development commitment. Cannot study at a centre in home country (with limited exceptions). Rotarians/close relatives ineligible.
opportunities.notes: Fits if Bob later works in conflict/health/humanitarian policy. Not suitable immediately after first year of undergrad.
opportunities.creative_angle: Accumulate NGO/UNV health-peace experience, then apply with a health-and-conflict thesis plan (e.g. Uppsala or Queensland).

opportunities.name: CEDARS Interest-Free Student Travel Loan
opportunities.what_you_get: Interest-free loan for academic-related overseas travel: typically HK$5,500–29,000 for exchange; HK$3,000–11,000 for other academic travel; repayable
opportunities.eligibility_summary: RESIDENCY TRAP: local students on full-time degree programmes only; priority to those without other university support and/or on Government TSFS; apply ≥14 working days before departure
opportunities.apply_window: Rolling; interview with student advising officer
opportunities.notes: Bridge financing, not a grant. Non-local students should use ROA/HKUWW/Laidlaw instead.
opportunities.creative_angle: Local students: use loan to cash-flow WHO/UN unpaid months while waiting for reimbursements.

opportunities.name: WHO Western Pacific Regional Office Internship & Volunteer Programme
opportunities.what_you_get: Regional internship exposure to WHO WPRO technical programmes; living-allowance rules follow WHO global internship policy (need-assessed); closer and often cheaper than Geneva for HK-based students
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Age ≥20; enrolled or recently graduated under WHO rules; completed 3 years university study; fluent in office language; WHO Member State passport; not previously in WHO IVP
opportunities.apply_window: Via WHO Stellis / WPRO career notices when posted
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Monitor WPRO careers page and WHO internship vacancies
opportunities.notes: Geographically strategic for HKU students; Asia-Pacific health security and health systems themes align with both of Bob’s fields.
opportunities.creative_angle: Target health systems, emergency preparedness, or governance units; emphasise East/Southeast Asia language or GBA experience.

opportunities.name: HKU Horizons Experiential Learning Beyond Hong Kong (Office Portfolio)
opportunities.what_you_get: Central portal for credit-bearing experiential programmes beyond Hong Kong plus associated financial support; includes Laidlaw and other Horizons-partnered opportunities; complements CEDARS and China Vision funding
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Primarily full-time HKU undergraduates; programme-specific rules vary; visiting students often excluded from funded scholars schemes
opportunities.notes: Use as the first stop each semester for newly announced funded short programmes rather than as a single award.
opportunities.creative_angle: Subscribe early and map every Horizons call against FOSS SIGC credits to avoid double-paying for experiences.

opportunities.name: Erasmus+ Traineeship (via Aalto TECH)
opportunities.what_you_get: Monthly Erasmus+ traineeship grant (country-band rates), possible green-travel top-up; Aalto processes applications for CHEM/TECH students; internship must earn ECTS toward the degree.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Aalto TECH (CHEM/ELEC/ENG/SCI) degree student; internship typically ≥2 months full-time in an Erasmus+ country; credits included in degree; usual Aalto grant rules (pace of studies, salary cap with grant, etc.).
opportunities.language_req: Working language of host (often English); Learning Agreement in English/Finnish as required by Aalto.
opportunities.gpa_hint: No fixed GPA; Aalto requires adequate study progress (~≥20 ECTS/semester average).
opportunities.year_of_study: Bachelor or Master (TECH); after sufficient credits.
opportunities.apply_window: Rolling; apply via Aalto online form ≥2 weeks before start
opportunities.notes: Primary EU mobility route for ChemE internships abroad while enrolled at Aalto. Cannot combine with another EU grant for the same period.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pair a self-sourced BASF/Neste/lab internship with Erasmus+ so salary + grant cover living costs.

opportunities.name: Aalto TECH International Internship & Travel Grant
opportunities.what_you_get: €1,500 internship grant plus destination-based travel grant (€400–€800) for non-Europe placements; thesis work abroad also eligible for the internship grant.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Aalto CHEM/ELEC/ENG/SCI student; credits for internship; ≥3 months (exceptions for short blended Erasmus); not previously received Aalto internship grant; destination not home/origin country.
opportunities.gpa_hint: Study-progress rule (~≥20 ECTS/semester), not a GPA cutoff.
opportunities.notes: Best for Canada/US/Asia ChemE internships when Erasmus+ does not apply; no Europe travel grant (use Erasmus+ instead).
opportunities.creative_angle: Stack with Mitacs Globalink or a paid overseas industrial internship for near-fully funded summers.

opportunities.name: Amgen Scholars Europe Programme
opportunities.funder: Amgen Foundation (hosts: Cambridge, ETH Zurich, Institut Pasteur, Karolinska, LMU Munich)
opportunities.what_you_get: 8–10 weeks research; stipend + housing + travel (details vary by host); Europe symposium (e.g. Cambridge).
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Undergraduate enrolled at a European (EHEA) university; bachelor not completed before programme ends; strong academics; interest in PhD; science/engineering preferred but related fields OK; apply separately to each host.
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Around 1 February annually (verify per host; LMU next cycle often opens 1 Nov)
opportunities.notes: Best ChemE angle: process/biotech/therapeutics-adjacent labs; ETH Zurich & Cambridge are highly competitive.
opportunities.creative_angle: Apply to multiple European hosts in one cycle; frame ChemE as bioprocess/formulation interest.

opportunities.name: HZB International Summer Student Programme (ISSP)
opportunities.what_you_get: 8-week paid research internship (~€1,900 gross subsistence for external students); ~75% travel subsidy (up to €200 EU / €800 non-EU); framework programme & lab project in photon science, photovoltaics, solar cells/catalysis, energy storage, materials, accelerators.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Bachelor/Master student (not PhD) in physics, chemistry, materials, engineering or related; ≥2 years full-time study; enrolled during programme; English; reference letter.
opportunities.gpa_hint: Competitive academic record; no fixed published cutoff.
opportunities.apply_window: Typically autumn (e.g. to mid-December for next summer)
opportunities.notes: Excellent ChemE fit via catalysis / electrochemical energy / materials projects.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use HZB for energy-transition ChemE CV before applying to Neste/BASF.

opportunities.name: ILL/ESRF International Student Summer Programme (X-Ray & Neutron Science)
opportunities.what_you_get: ~4-week programme with experimental project in ILL or ESRF group + lectures; financial support and free accommodation for selected students (~20 places).
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Undergraduate enrolled at a university in an ESRF or ILL member/associate country (Finland and Poland qualify via membership); strong English (B2+/C1).
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Often late February / mid-March (e.g. 28 Feb 2026 for 2026 edition)
opportunities.notes: Great for ChemE materials/soft-matter/catalysis characterisation interest; Finland & Poland are in the European neutron/synchrotron ecosystem.
opportunities.creative_angle: Position as training for catalysis/process materials characterisation careers.

opportunities.name: CERN Technical Studentship (incl. Material & Surface Science)
opportunities.what_you_get: 4–12 month contract; monthly net allowance (~CHF 3,486); health insurance; travel support depending on circumstances; 30 days leave + annual closure.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: National of CERN Member/Associate Member State (Finland & Poland qualify); ≥18 months (3 semesters) completed; remain enrolled; good English or French; PhD not eligible.
opportunities.apply_window: Multiple selection rounds per year via careers.cern
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Rolling calls — check open Technical Student vacancies
opportunities.notes: Material & Surface Science and related engineering streams are the best ChemE entry points.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use for a funded semester+ industry-style engineering placement recognized for ECTS via Aalto.

opportunities.name: Max Planck Summer Internship Program (MaxSIP)
opportunities.funder: Max Planck Institute of Biochemistry & MPI for Biological Intelligence (Martinsried)
opportunities.what_you_get: Up to ~10 weeks full-time lab internship (mid-Jun–end-Sep window); funding/support details verify annually on MaxSIP site; highly competitive (~tens of places / thousands of apps).
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Bachelor (year 2+) or Master in biology, chemistry, computational biology, biophysics, neuroscience or related; some research experience preferred; enrolled during internship (or recently graduated per call text).
opportunities.apply_window: Typically autumn–early winter (platform often opens ~1 Feb window historically; verify)
opportunities.notes: Funding package should be confirmed each year; extremely selective.
opportunities.creative_angle: Target biochemistry / catalysis-adjacent MPI labs if ChemE profile is molecular.

opportunities.name: Max Planck School Matter to Life — Undergraduate Research Opportunities (MtL-URO)
opportunities.funder: Max Planck School Matter to Life
opportunities.what_you_get: 10-week research internship; travel, accommodation, and living allowance covered.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Bachelor students in physics, chemistry, biology, informatics, materials, engineering or related; GPA at least 1.7 (German scale); enrolled before and throughout internship; portal typically from 1 August.
opportunities.gpa_hint: ≥1.7 German scale (convert carefully from Finnish grades).
opportunities.year_of_study: Bachelor only (must remain undergrad during internship)
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Verify annually — applications typically late summer/autumn for next year
opportunities.notes: Also a pipeline into MtL integrated MSc/PhD (later).
opportunities.creative_angle: Use as funded soft-matter / origin-of-life / molecular engineering bridge from ChemE.

opportunities.name: EDUFI Traineeship Abroad
destinations: Worldwide (Finnish missions, cultural institutes, Team Finland Knowledge Network, Finnish language departments, etc.)
opportunities.what_you_get: EDUFI traineeship grant for a curated placement; application rounds twice yearly; placements listed per call.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Finnish citizen OR permanent residence in Finland; full-time HE student (or recent graduate within 1 year); age ≥18; Finnish language required for most posts; cannot combine with Erasmus+ for same placement; once-in-a-lifetime EDUFI/CIMO traineeship grant.
opportunities.language_req: Finnish for most placements; limited options without Finnish.
opportunities.gpa_hint: Not GPA-based; selection by host + EDUFI criteria.
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Spring and autumn rounds — check oph.fi each cycle
opportunities.notes: Maja’s Finnish citizenship is the eligibility key; ChemE-relevant posts are fewer than for humanities/IR — scan Team Finland / tech-related lists.
opportunities.creative_angle: Look for science/tech diplomacy or Team Finland Knowledge Network placements near chemical industry hubs.

opportunities.name: Nordplus Higher Education Mobility
opportunities.what_you_get: Mobility grants via institutional Nordplus networks (study, express mobility, sometimes placements); rates include travel lumpsums and weekly/monthly stipends depending on activity.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Student at a Nordic/Baltic HEI participating in a Nordplus network; mobility arranged through home institution international office / network coordinator (Aalto).
opportunities.language_req: English or host language depending on course.
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Verify with Aalto international office / Nordplus network annually
opportunities.notes: Student mobility often secondary to Erasmus+; useful for short intensive courses or Nordic ChemE partners when Erasmus months are used up.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use for short Baltic/Nordic lab courses when a full Erasmus semester is not needed.

opportunities.name: BASF Explore Together (International Internship) & Paid BASF Internships
destinations: BASF sites worldwide (Explore Together: outside Germany); Germany sites via regular portal
opportunities.what_you_get: Paid internship (2–6 months); Explore Together covers visa + flight and local internship salary (living costs may still need top-up in some countries); European Talent Pool pathway.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Enrolled university student for entire internship; strong academics; ChemE/chemistry/engineering preferred for process roles; apply via BASF job portal (Explore Together keyword) or unsolicited to subsidiaries.
opportunities.language_req: English; German useful for Ludwigshafen/Germany sites.
opportunities.notes: Combine with Erasmus+ (Europe) or Aalto TECH grant (outside Europe) if salary needs topping up — check double-funding rules.
opportunities.creative_angle: Apply Explore Together for a funded non-Germany site, then use Aalto Erasmus+/TECH grant where allowed.

opportunities.name: Neste Student / Summer Trainee Positions
destinations: selected international Neste locations when posted
opportunities.what_you_get: Paid summer trainee / internship salary (industry rates); onboarding and supervision; strong ChemE relevance (refining, renewables, process analytics).
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Typically enrolled students in relevant fields (ChemE, chemistry, process, data); Finnish/English as required per posting; Aalto–Neste strategic partnership increases visibility of ChemE projects.
opportunities.language_req: English and/or Finnish depending on role.
opportunities.gpa_hint: Competitive industry hiring; solid transcript helps.
opportunities.apply_window: Peak autumn–winter for next summer traineeships
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Verify annually on Neste careers (postings open/close continuously)
opportunities.notes: Often Finland-based (not abroad); still a funded ChemE industry path; useful before outbound mobility.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use Neste summer + Fortum and Neste Foundation later for thesis/energy research grants.

opportunities.name: Fortum and Neste Foundation Grants
destinations: Finland (research/education support; mobility possible via project budget)
opportunities.what_you_get: Competitive grants for energy-sector research/education (climate mitigation, energy systems, transport alternatives, bioeconomy/circular economy).
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Applicants in natural/technical/economic sciences linked to energy; check annual call text for student vs researcher eligibility.
opportunities.year_of_study: Often Master thesis / doctoral; verify call (may include advanced students)
opportunities.notes: More thesis/research-oriented than a summer internship; excellent for renewable ChemE topics.
opportunities.creative_angle: Align Master’s thesis on sustainable fuels/process electrification with foundation priorities.

opportunities.name: EFCE Student Mobility Award
opportunities.what_you_get: Biennial cash prizes for top European ChemE students who studied successfully ≥1 semester in each of two different countries; presented at ECCE conference.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: European chemical engineering students with documented successful study mobility in two countries (1st or 2nd cycle).
opportunities.year_of_study: Bachelor/Master ChemE after dual-country study periods
opportunities.notes: Not a funding-to-go programme; rewards mobility already done — plan Erasmus + second country stay early.
opportunities.creative_angle: Design a Finland+another-EU ChemE path specifically to become award-eligible.

opportunities.name: AIChE International Student Chapter Leadership Development Travel Grant
destinations: United States (AIChE Annual Student Conference location)
opportunities.what_you_get: Up to ~8 grants: roundtrip airfare, complimentary ASC registration, hotel accommodations.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Active AIChE undergrad member; past/current leader of an international (non-US/Canada) student chapter; ESC involvement required per current rules; advisor nomination; chapter annual report requirements.
opportunities.notes: Requires active AIChE international chapter leadership path at Aalto/region — start early.
opportunities.creative_angle: Build/lead an AIChE student chapter presence in Finland to unlock US conference travel funding.

opportunities.name: AIChE Chem-E-Car Competition
opportunities.funder: AIChE (prizes sponsored; travel usually via university/sponsors)
opportunities.what_you_get: Regional/national cash prizes (e.g. finals $2000/$1000/$500); prestige; travel not automatically funded by AIChE.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: ChemE student teams designing chemically powered cars; compete regionally then finals by invitation.
opportunities.notes: Funding is prize + local sponsorship; combine with Aalto student org/travel grants for competition travel.
opportunities.creative_angle: Form an Aalto Chem-E-Car team and seek guild/corporate sponsors for travel.

opportunities.name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master in Sustainable Catalysis (SuCat)
opportunities.what_you_get: Competitive EMJM scholarships (tuition + living/travel components when awarded); 120 ECTS double/joint master across catalysis specialisations; industry-linked internship/thesis.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Bachelor (~180 ECTS) in chemistry or similar; English ≥B2 (C1 preferred); worldwide applicants; apply via master-sucat.eu.
opportunities.gpa_hint: Strong chemistry bachelor; selective consortium ranking.
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: February annually (portal often reopens late year)
opportunities.notes: Best as post-Bachelor path; EU nationals remain competitive for scholarships but EMJM slots are limited.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use Aalto ChemE + research summer (HZB/Amgen) as SuCat scholarship package.

opportunities.name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master MULTIPHASE (Multiphase Systems)
opportunities.what_you_get: EMJM scholarship opportunities; dual/triple degrees in chemical/process engineering & materials; industrial associated partners; green-line research project & thesis internship.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Strong Bachelor in chemical/process engineering or closely related; English proficiency; competitive international admission.
opportunities.notes: Strong classic ChemE/process focus vs SuCat’s catalysis focus.
opportunities.creative_angle: Ideal if Maja prefers multiphase reactors / particles / process intensification over molecular catalysis.

opportunities.name: EIT InnoEnergy Masters+ Scholarships / Fee Waivers
opportunities.what_you_get: Merit-based full/partial fee waivers (e.g. European partial waiver reducing annual fee); automatic consideration during admission; energy-innovation focus.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Bachelor (or final-year) in engineering/science relevant to energy programmes; English requirements per track; European university graduates eligible for certain European fee-waiver categories.
opportunities.apply_window: Multiple admission rounds; earlier rounds better for full waivers
opportunities.notes: Not a summer programme; Master’s pathway for energy ChemE (sustainable fuels, process energy).
opportunities.creative_angle: Combine with Neste internship experience for Energy Impact Scholarship narrative.

opportunities.name: ASLA-Fulbright Graduate Grant (Finland → USA)
opportunities.what_you_get: Top-up grant typically USD 5k–20k (sometimes more) plus IIE placement support toward US university funding packages for Master’s/graduate study.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Finnish citizenship and residence; not Finnish–US dual citizen / no US permanent residence; seeking US graduate study; limited awards per year.
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Verify fulbright.fi each year (often spring–summer for next AY)
opportunities.notes: Uses Finnish citizenship track; Polish citizenship alone is not the Fulbright Finland route.
opportunities.creative_angle: Target US ChemE/process energy Master’s with university assistantship + Fulbright top-up.

opportunities.name: Foundation for Aalto University Science and Technology — Thesis Grants
opportunities.funder: Foundation for Aalto University Science and Technology
destinations: Finland (thesis funding; research visits via own-funds calls for doctoral level)
opportunities.what_you_get: Industry-donated MSc thesis grants (example scale: large donations can fund ~€14.8k for a 6-month Master’s thesis); continuous monthly decisions for donated funds; separate spring own-funds call mainly for doctoral students/research visits.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Aalto Schools of Technology students (CHEM eligible) writing MSc/DSc thesis aligned with donor field; student applies after gift is available.
opportunities.apply_window: Ongoing for industry-gift thesis grants; spring for own-funds doctoral calls
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Rolling for MSc thesis gifts; spring for doctoral own-funds
opportunities.notes: Not outbound travel per se, but can fund a thesis period that includes industry/abroad collaboration components when structured with supervisor.
opportunities.creative_angle: Seek a CHEM donor-funded thesis with Neste/chemical-industry gift streams.

opportunities.name: Zawacka NAWA (Outgoing Bilateral Mobility)
opportunities.funder: Polish National Agency for Academic Exchange (NAWA)
destinations: Partner countries under Polish bilateral agreements (e.g. Czechia, Slovakia, Japan, Taiwan, China, Mexico, etc. — list varies by year)
opportunities.what_you_get: Scholarship for part of studies / research stay / partner-country summer language course per bilateral offer; funding from NAWA Director and/or host side.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Typically students enrolled at a Polish HEI (or recent Polish graduates per call); Polish citizenship common path — Aalto-only enrollment usually does not qualify.
opportunities.apply_window: Annual (e.g. to late January for next AY)
opportunities.notes: Polish citizenship helps only if she can apply via a Polish university affiliation; otherwise closed while solely at Aalto.
opportunities.creative_angle: If she does a Polish double-enrolment/guest semester, Zawacka could unlock non-Erasmus destinations.

opportunities.name: Erasmus+ Studies (Aalto Exchange Partners)
destinations: Aalto CHEM/TECH partner universities across Europe (and limited worldwide partners)
opportunities.what_you_get: Semester/year exchange with Erasmus+ monthly grant + travel support bands; course recognition toward Aalto degree; green travel top-ups possible.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Aalto degree student selected via school exchange process; enough ECTS completed; Learning Agreement; max 12 Erasmus+ months per study cycle.
opportunities.language_req: English or host language per partner.
opportunities.gpa_hint: School selection criteria apply (often competitive for popular destinations).
opportunities.year_of_study: Usually after 1st year Bachelor or during Master
opportunities.apply_window: Aalto exchange application rounds (typically annual main call)
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Verify Aalto CHEM outgoing exchange deadlines annually
opportunities.notes: Core outbound mobility tool; pair with EFCE Student Mobility Award strategy (two-country study).
opportunities.creative_angle: Choose a strong ChemE partner (Delft/TUM/etc.) then add a second-country traineeship for EFCE eligibility.

opportunities.name: ETH Zurich Student Summer Research Fellowship (Computer Science)
opportunities.funder: ETH Zurich Department of Computer Science
opportunities.what_you_get: Competitive 2-month summer research fellowship in CS areas (stipend/details on ETH SSRF pages).
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Undergraduate/graduate students worldwide except ETH-enrolled; CS-focused — not a ChemE programme.
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Verify annually on inf.ethz.ch SSRF page
opportunities.notes: Often confused with a general ETH summer; ChemE students should prefer Amgen@ETH or direct lab contact unless doing process-systems/computation ChemE.
opportunities.creative_angle: Only pursue if Maja has a strong computational ChemE / ML-for-process profile.

opportunities.name: First-in-the-Family Education Fund (FIFE)
opportunities.what_you_get: Up to HK$20,000 grant for study abroad, service-learning, internships, or conference travel
opportunities.eligibility_summary: First-generation UG (neither parent nor grandparent attended university); financial need considered; open to non-locals
opportunities.gpa_hint: Not specified; proposal + faculty sponsor letter
opportunities.notes: Powerful stacking layer if first-gen criterion met; underpublicised.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pair FIFE with HKUWW or a summer research shortfall (airfare gap).

opportunities.name: HKU Summer Research Programme
opportunities.what_you_get: 10-week supervised research; HK$10,000 completion scholarship; airfare/accommodation support for non-local participants
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG/PG worldwide including current HKU students; competitive selection
opportunities.notes: Builds poster/paper foundation for later prestige awards.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use as paid HKU summer research when external Amgen/ETH slots miss.

opportunities.name: HKU CDS Research Internship Programme
opportunities.funder: HKU Faculty of Science / Centre for Data Science
opportunities.what_you_get: ~HK$19,601 stipend for 10-week computational biology / biomedical AI internship
opportunities.eligibility_summary: ~40 outstanding UG selected; strong computational background; HKU and visiting students
opportunities.notes: Higher stipend than many HKU summer options; best if computational BME.
opportunities.creative_angle: Target medical imaging AI / bioinformatics streams for BME narrative.

opportunities.name: HKU Service 100 & Common Core Service-Learning Trips
opportunities.funder: HKU Gallant Ho Experiential Learning Centre + Common Core
opportunities.what_you_get: Subsidised/fully funded 1–3 week service-learning trips; full subsidies for demonstrated need
opportunities.eligibility_summary: All UG; selection via application; some trips have course prerequisites
opportunities.notes: Strong global-health / low-resource medtech narrative fuel.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pick medical-device repair or rural-health engineering tracks.

opportunities.name: Amgen Scholars Program — US & Europe
opportunities.what_you_get: Fully funded summer research: airfare, accommodation, stipend, symposium
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG in life sciences/BME/biotech; US hosts vary (some US-enrolled only); Europe generally open to non-EU
opportunities.notes: Distinct from Amgen Asia already in DB; verify each host eligibility carefully.
opportunities.creative_angle: Prioritise Cambridge/ETH/LMU hosts over US-restricted ones.

opportunities.name: Stanford Bio-X Undergraduate Summer Research Program
opportunities.what_you_get: 10-week research; US$7,500 stipend; housing subsidy; travel allowance; international students eligible
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG in life sciences/BME/bioengineering; ≥1 year completed
opportunities.notes: Explicitly open to international students; strong medtech narrative fit.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pitch neural interfaces / medical imaging AI lab match.

opportunities.name: Charité BIH Summer School (Berlin)
opportunities.funder: Charité – Universitätsmedizin Berlin + Berlin Institute of Health
opportunities.what_you_get: 8–10 week research; ~€800–1,000/month stipend; accommodation assistance; some travel support
opportunities.eligibility_summary: International UG/PG in biomedical fields
opportunities.notes: May need HKU ROA/FIFE top-up; strong EU PhD network.
opportunities.creative_angle: Stack with German lab visit + St. Gallen or EMBC Europe trip.

opportunities.name: Karolinska Institutet Summer Research School
opportunities.what_you_get: 8-week research; tax-free stipend ~SEK 12,000/month; some travel support
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG at any university; international applicants welcome; merit-based
opportunities.notes: Nobel-institution brand; neuroscience/CMB labs most relevant.
opportunities.creative_angle: Align with SI Scholarship narrative for later Sweden master's.

opportunities.name: Utrecht University Summer School + EU Summer Schools
opportunities.what_you_get: 1–6 week courses; scholarships may waive tuition + contribute accommodation/travel
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Open enrollment many courses; scholarship application for tuition waiver
opportunities.notes: Comparable schools at KU Leuven, Copenhagen, Amsterdam, Groningen.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pick Neuroimaging / Biomedical Data Science tracks; stack ROA.

opportunities.name: Tsinghua iCampus & SJTU Visiting Student Programme
opportunities.funder: Tsinghua University / Shanghai Jiao Tong University
opportunities.what_you_get: 4–12 week lab placement; subsidised accommodation; some stipend; tuition waiver
opportunities.eligibility_summary: International UG; pre-arranged lab host required
opportunities.notes: Strong network asset for Schwarzman/Yenching.
opportunities.creative_angle: Secure Tsinghua BME or SJTU Med-X host before applying.

opportunities.name: KAIST Visiting Student + GIST Korea Summer
opportunities.what_you_get: KAIST: subsidised housing + stipend ~KRW 800k–1.2M; GIST: tuition, housing, stipend, partial travel
opportunities.eligibility_summary: International UG; HKU students welcome
opportunities.notes: GIST more generously funded; geographic proximity from HK.
opportunities.creative_angle: Korea summer research then iGEM Paris in autumn.

opportunities.name: Chevening Scholarship (UK)
opportunities.what_you_get: 1-year UK master's: tuition, living stipend ~£1,500–1,800/mo, airfare, thesis grant
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Kazakh citizens resident in KZ at application; bachelor's; ≥2 years (2,800 hrs) work experience; 2-year return commitment
opportunities.notes: Cannot apply immediately after HKU; plan 2-year work gap.
opportunities.creative_angle: Work in clinical engineering/RA then target Imperial/UCL bioengineering MSc.

opportunities.name: MEXT Scholarship (Japan)
opportunities.what_you_get: Full tuition, stipend ~¥143–145k/mo (research students), round-trip airfare, housing assistance
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Kazakh citizens via Embassy Recommendation (Astana); under 35 for research category; bachelor's required
opportunities.notes: Embassy path; target UTokyo/Kyoto/Tohoku BME.
opportunities.creative_angle: Apply final HKU year for research-student April start.

opportunities.name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters (EMJM / EMMBIOME)
opportunities.what_you_get: 2-year joint master's: tuition, stipend €1,000–1,400/mo, travel €2–4k/year, installation €1,000, insurance
opportunities.eligibility_summary: All nationalities; bachelor's; Partner Country quota (Kazakhstan eligible)
opportunities.notes: EMMBIOME and MemBioMed highlighted for BME; apply to multiple EMJM in parallel.
opportunities.creative_angle: Lead with EMMBIOME; also MemBioMed at Côte d'Azur.

opportunities.name: Fulbright Foreign Student Program (US)
opportunities.what_you_get: US master's/research: tuition, stipend, airfare, insurance, book allowance
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Kazakh citizens resident in KZ; bachelor's + ≥2 years work; English proficiency; no US PR/citizenship
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Early Sep via US Embassy Astana
opportunities.notes: Same work-experience gate as Chevening.
opportunities.creative_angle: Target Hopkins/Duke/Georgia Tech BME after industry years.

opportunities.name: Swiss Government Excellence Scholarship
opportunities.funder: Swiss Federal Commission for Scholarships for Foreign Students
opportunities.what_you_get: Master's/PhD/research: CHF 1,920/mo stipend, tuition waiver, insurance, housing subsidy, airfare
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Kazakh citizens via Swiss Embassy; bachelor's for master's; host admission letter required
opportunities.notes: Natural follow-on after ETH/EPFL summer research.
opportunities.creative_angle: Target ETH D-BSSE or EPFL Engineering with prior Swiss summer letter.

opportunities.name: Stipendium Hungaricum
opportunities.what_you_get: Tuition waiver, stipend HUF 40–140k, dorm/housing contribution, medical insurance
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Kazakh citizens via bilateral quota; bachelor's for master's track
opportunities.notes: Dedicated KZ quota improves odds vs global awards.
opportunities.creative_angle: BME at Budapest University of Technology and Economics.

opportunities.name: SI / Eiffel / Orange Tulip Scholarships (Sweden, France, Netherlands)
opportunities.funder: Swedish Institute; French government; Dutch universities/Neso
opportunities.what_you_get: SI: tuition + SEK 12k/mo + travel; Eiffel: €1,181–1,700/mo + travel/insurance; OTS: often full tuition + living (programme-specific); Holland Scholarship €5k one-off
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Kazakh citizens eligible; programme/age/field restrictions vary
opportunities.apply_window: Sweden Dec–Feb; France Oct–Jan; NL Oct–Apr
opportunities.notes: Bundle of national EU awards from PDF §3.8/3.15; apply in parallel.
opportunities.creative_angle: SI→KTH BME; Eiffel→Paris-Saclay/Polytechnique; OTS→TU Delft BME.

opportunities.name: Rhodes Scholarship (Oxford)
opportunities.what_you_get: 1–3 years Oxford PG: tuition, stipend ~£18k, airfare, Rhodes House accommodation
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Kazakh citizens via Rhodes Global constituency; age under 27 (some under 25); bachelor's
opportunities.notes: Needs prior funding wins (Laidlaw, Amgen, exchange) in narrative.
opportunities.creative_angle: MSc Biomedical Engineering / Integrated Immunology framing.

opportunities.name: Gates Cambridge Scholarship
opportunities.what_you_get: MPhil/PhD: tuition, stipend ~£18–20k, airfare, academic development fund
opportunities.eligibility_summary: All nationalities except UK; any full-time Cambridge PG degree
opportunities.apply_window: Concurrent with Cambridge (Dec US / Jan international)
opportunities.notes: Slightly more accessible than Rhodes (~80/year).
opportunities.creative_angle: MPhil Bioengineering / Computational Biology + prior summer research.

opportunities.name: Knight-Hennessy Scholars (Stanford)
opportunities.what_you_get: Any Stanford graduate degree: tuition, living stipend ~US$50k/year, travel, enrichment fund
opportunities.eligibility_summary: All nationalities; bachelor's; apply within ~7 years of undergrad graduation
opportunities.notes: ~5% acceptance; Bioengineering MSc/PhD natural fit.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pair Stanford Bio-X summer (if won) with KH narrative.

opportunities.name: Hong Kong PhD Fellowship Scheme (HKPFS)
opportunities.what_you_get: HK$331,200/year stipend + HK$13,800/year conference travel for 3 years; tuition waived
opportunities.eligibility_summary: All nationalities; new full-time PhD at 8 UGC HK universities
opportunities.notes: Built-in annual conference travel; natural HKU BME PhD path.
opportunities.creative_angle: HKU BME PhD + HKPFS → Lindau in PhD Y1–2.

opportunities.name: Jardine Foundation Scholarship (Oxford/Cambridge)
opportunities.what_you_get: MPhil/PhD Oxbridge: tuition, accommodation, living stipend, travel, books
opportunities.eligibility_summary: All nationalities; Asia-connected preference; resident in Asia at application; merit + leadership
opportunities.notes: Apply while resident in HK/KZ; PDF also mentions Jardine-HKU UG scholarship separately.
opportunities.creative_angle: Asia-focused health-tech career narrative + HKU degree.

opportunities.name: Lindau Nobel Laureate Meetings
opportunities.funder: Lindau Nobel Laureate Council + academic partners
opportunities.what_you_get: 1-week meeting; ~5 nights accommodation; travel reimbursement up to ~€1,000
opportunities.eligibility_summary: PhD students / early postdocs under 35; HKU nomination or open application
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Verify for annual meeting field (2026 interdisciplinary)
opportunities.notes: Plan after HKPFS PhD start; not for current undergrads.
opportunities.creative_angle: Interdisciplinary year ideal for BME; nominate via HKU.

opportunities.name: UN Youth Delegate Programme
opportunities.what_you_get: Travel + accommodation funded by national mission for UNGA; possible additional appearances
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Typically age 18–24; Kazakh citizen; national MFA selection process
opportunities.notes: KZ periodically sends delegates; health-tech policy angle fits BME.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pitch AMR / medical-device access youth statement.

opportunities.name: Youth Future Summit (Geneva)
opportunities.what_you_get: 30 fully funded seats: airfare, 4-star hotel, meals, visa support, UN/WTO HQ visits
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Age 18–35; leadership/SDG engagement; competitive
opportunities.notes: Geneva location adjacent to WHO/Gavi networks.
opportunities.creative_angle: Combine with WHO internship scouting or EPFL side visit.

opportunities.name: HPAIR (Harvard Project for Asian & International Relations)
opportunities.what_you_get: Travel grants for Asia delegates to Harvard Conference; Asia Conference subsidised registration + some grants
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG/PG worldwide; competitive application
opportunities.apply_window: ~4 months before Feb Harvard / Aug Asia conferences
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Oct for Feb; Apr for Aug (approx)
opportunities.notes: Health/Technology tracks relevant; early CV builder.
opportunities.creative_angle: Asia Conference from HK + ROA stacking for Harvard Conference grant.

opportunities.name: St. Gallen Symposium (Wings of Excellence)
opportunities.funder: University of St. Gallen + student initiative
opportunities.what_you_get: Winning essay: airfare, accommodation, full symposium access (registration waived for student delegates)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG/PG worldwide; Wings of Excellence essay competition for funded places
opportunities.notes: Essay is the funded entry gate.
opportunities.creative_angle: Health-tech / technology governance essay track.

opportunities.name: BMES Annual Meeting Travel Awards
opportunities.what_you_get: ~US$500–1,000 student travel awards for presenters
opportunities.eligibility_summary: BMES student members; accepted abstract; some diversity awards
opportunities.apply_window: Abstract Mar–Apr; travel award May–Jun; meeting Oct
opportunities.notes: Distinct from DB CMBE-specific travel grants.
opportunities.creative_angle: Capstone abstract + ROA/FIFE stack for full US trip.

opportunities.name: Citadel Conference Travel Grant
opportunities.what_you_get: STEM conference travel grants of varying amounts
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Enrolled bachelor/master/PhD in STEM; conference participation required
opportunities.notes: Lightweight application; stacks well with society travel grants.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use as registration/meals layer with IEEE EMBS + ROA.

opportunities.name: Clinton Global Initiative University (CGI U)
opportunities.what_you_get: Registration waived; partial travel via Commitment to Action; some fully funded places
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG/PG worldwide; must develop a Commitment to Action project
opportunities.notes: Launch low-resource medical-device Commitment to Action.
opportunities.creative_angle: Medtech-for-LMIC project → follow-on foundation funding.

opportunities.name: AIESEC Global Talent
opportunities.what_you_get: Paid internships 3–18 months; salary covers local living; airfare/visa usually self-funded (stack ROA/FIFE)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG, recent grads, PG; relevant skills for placement
opportunities.notes: HKU has AIESEC chapter; Global Volunteer is unpaid housing/meals variant.
opportunities.creative_angle: German medtech / Singapore health-tech / Japan biomedical firm.

opportunities.name: Operation Smile Medical Missions
opportunities.what_you_get: Accommodation + meals during 1–2 week mission; some travel support; biomedical technician volunteer roles
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Medical and non-medical volunteers; HKU student chapter available
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Rolling via volunteer portal / HKU chapter
opportunities.notes: Rare clinical low-resource BME exposure.
opportunities.creative_angle: Equipment maintenance volunteer → scholarship narrative fuel.

opportunities.name: Engineers Without Borders (EWB) HKU Chapter
opportunities.what_you_get: Project-based international travel; some placements fully funded
opportunities.eligibility_summary: HKU students; engineering and BME welcome
opportunities.notes: Valued by postgraduate scholarship committees.
opportunities.creative_angle: Rural clinic medical-equipment maintenance projects.

opportunities.name: Singapore International Graduate Award (SINGA)
opportunities.what_you_get: PhD: full tuition + S$2,000–2,500/month stipend
opportunities.eligibility_summary: All nationalities (ASEAN UG scholarship closed to Kazakh; SINGA PhD open)
opportunities.notes: From PDF §3.12; ASEAN UG track not eligible for Kazakh nationals.
opportunities.creative_angle: NGS integrative sciences / biomedical engineering PhD.

opportunities.name: KAUST Fellowship (MS/PhD)
opportunities.funder: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology
opportunities.what_you_get: Full tuition, stipend ~US$2–3k/mo, free housing, medical/dental, relocation
opportunities.eligibility_summary: All nationalities; bachelor's for MS; competitive
opportunities.notes: Distinct from KAUST VSRP already in DB.
opportunities.creative_angle: Bioscience/bioengineering track after UG summers.

opportunities.name: Taiwan MOE Scholarship
opportunities.what_you_get: Monthly stipend NT$15–25k + tuition waiver up to NT$40k/semester (UG 4y / master's 2y)
opportunities.eligibility_summary: International students including Kazakh; apply via TECO (Moscow handles KZ)
opportunities.notes: Distinct from Taiwan Huayu Enrichment (language) already in DB.
opportunities.creative_angle: NTU/NTHU/NYCU biomedical engineering master's.

opportunities.name: DAAD Master's Scholarship (Germany / EPOS)
opportunities.what_you_get: €934/mo stipend, travel, insurance, study/rent subsidy; tuition typically free
opportunities.eligibility_summary: All nationalities; bachelor's; 2 years work preferred for some EPOS programmes
opportunities.notes: DB has RISE + DAAD KZ database; this is degree-level award.
opportunities.creative_angle: RWTH Aachen / TUM Medical Engineering EPOS tracks.

opportunities.name: Workaway (Work-Exchange)
opportunities.what_you_get: Accommodation + meals for 4–5 hrs work/day; ~US$54/year membership; airfare self-funded
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Age 18+; no qualifications required
opportunities.notes: Accommodation layer for stacking with conference/research trips; WWOOF/Worldpackers similar.
opportunities.creative_angle: Extend funded conference weeks with free EU accommodation.

opportunities.name: Hong Kong Techathon+
opportunities.what_you_get: Cross-university product/startup sprint with themed tracks (AI, ESG, life science, smart city); mentorship/mixers; fast-track to HKSTP Ideation (up to HK$100k seed) and university startup programmes; finale pitch at Science Park.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Tertiary students (local + overseas/non-local streams); individuals or teams; open via campus/HKSTP channels.
opportunities.language_req: English (primary); Chinese also common in mixers
opportunities.apply_window: Typically Sep–Oct for Jan finale (e.g. Sep–Oct 2025 for Jan 2026)
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Watch Sep–Oct annually for next cycle
opportunities.notes: Flagship since 2015; strongest HK bridge from weekend build → funded ideation. Local + international tracks.
