opportunities.creative_angle: Treat it as a 4-month venture studio: use campus mixers for customer discovery, then pitch a BME-flavoured health/ESG prototype into Ideation.
opportunities.why_great: Best recurring HK funnel from interdisciplinary product build to real seed funding and incubation—ideal for design + entrepreneurship practice with industry mentors.

opportunities.name: Cathay Hackathon
opportunities.what_you_get: Masterclasses; 24-hour development day; industry pitches; cash/prizes; fast-track to Cathay Digital & IT internship/graduate programmes; GBA exposure (recent finals in Shenzhen).
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG/PG students and recent graduates (within ~2 years); teams of 3–5 or individuals matched; aged 16+.
opportunities.language_req: Choose English, Cantonese, or Putonghua for all submissions
opportunities.notes: Annual since 2016; aviation/travel/ops challenges; roles include Product Owner, Designer, Developer.
opportunities.creative_angle: Frame a passenger-health / accessibility / ops product with clear UX journey and measurable airline KPI.
opportunities.why_great: Real corporate problem statements plus designer/product-owner tracks make it excellent practice for shipping a pitchable product under industry constraints.

opportunities.name: IOT Data Hackathon
opportunities.what_you_get: Workshops; real challenge-provider data; mentorship; Hack Day + Final Pitch; cash awards; fast-track to Cyberport CCMF/Incubation and HKSTP Ideation; internship opportunities for student winners.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Student stream: HK tertiary students (campus in/out of HK); Professional stream: HK startups/professionals (HK resident or HK company member required).
opportunities.apply_window: Typically Nov–Dec (e.g. deadline 20 Dec 2025 for 2026 edition)
opportunities.notes: Multi-month programme with IoT/open/alternative data themes: tech-business innovation + social impact/sustainability.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pair sensor/IoT data with a BME or public-health use case and a clear go-to-market for a challenge partner.
opportunities.why_great: Hands-on data-to-prototype path with incubation fast-tracks—strong for product validation and entrepreneurship follow-through.

opportunities.name: Hong Kong Web3 Ideathon
opportunities.what_you_get: Cyberport workshop; proposal → finalist pitch; cash prizes (HK$30k/20k/10k + honourable mentions); Cyberport entrepreneurship programme fast-track interviews; Web3/AI/fintech literacy network.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Full-time tertiary students in Hong Kong; teams of 2–4.
opportunities.notes: Tracks often include financial literacy, investment/insurance decisions, scam/fraud safeguarding with AI/Web3.
opportunities.creative_angle: Build a trust/UX-first fintech literacy product—prototype optional but strengthens technical feasibility score.
opportunities.why_great: Forces problem framing, business vision, and pitch craft around real consumer finance pain—great entrepreneurship reps without requiring deep crypto coding.

opportunities.name: techstars Startup Weekend Hong Kong (for Social Good)
opportunities.funder: HKBU CISL / TriAngle + Alibaba Cloud (Techstars licensed)
opportunities.what_you_get: 54-hour startup sprint; GenAI prototyping support; mentors/judges; winning teams previously advanced to Alibaba Yunqi Conference; social-enterprise skill building.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: University students (HKBU and other institutions welcome; recent editions asked ≥2 HKBU members per team—verify each year).
opportunities.notes: Classic Startup Weekend format focused on SDGs/social good; 2025 ran 21–23 Mar at HKBU + Alibaba HK HQ.
opportunities.creative_angle: Arrive with a customer problem (not a product); use weekend for discovery experiments + no/low-code MVP.
opportunities.why_great: Pure entrepreneurship gym: idea → validate → prototype → pitch in 54 hours with mentor loops—best format for learning venture craft.

opportunities.name: Inter-University GenAI Hackathon for the SDGs
opportunities.funder: Alliance of HK’s 8 UGC universities (supported by UGC, Alibaba Cloud)
opportunities.what_you_get: Extended multi-week hackathon; seminars/workshops; cross-university teams; prizes; pathways to HKSTP and other entrepreneurship events.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: UG/PG students from HKU, CityUHK, HKUST, HKBU, CUHK, PolyU, LingU, EdUHK; free; all majors.
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Sep annually via university registration links
opportunities.notes: Not a 48-hour sprint—designed for deeper collaboration on inclusive cities, food/waste, ageing, culture, etc.
opportunities.creative_angle: Lead as product designer/PM: ship a GenAI service for ageing or accessibility with a clear SDG impact metric.
opportunities.why_great: Low barrier for non-CS students, multi-week build time, and explicit incubation pathways—excellent for thoughtful product + social entrepreneurship.

opportunities.name: MedTech Hackathon GBA
opportunities.funder: HKU Techno-Entrepreneur Core (TEC) / TEA + Bay Charm
opportunities.what_you_get: Lean Launcher training; GBA industry visits (Guangzhou/Suzhou); investor networking; ASGH pitch exposure; cash awards; Cambridge Ignite fee subsidies for top HKU teams.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Multidisciplinary teams; ≥1 HKU-affiliated member (professor/researcher/alumni/staff); ASGH/Lean Launcher component needs ≥1 HKU researcher with PhD min.
opportunities.year_of_study: Researchers, postgrads, alumni founders (not typical UG-only)
opportunities.apply_window: Winter–spring (e.g. application deadline 22 Mar 2026 listed on HKUMed TTU)
opportunities.notes: Commercialisation-focused MedTech programme more than weekend coding; strong GBA immersion.
opportunities.creative_angle: If you can join/partner an HKU lab team, own the product–market narrative and clinical workflow design.
opportunities.why_great: Rare GBA MedTech commercialisation loop: lean training, hospital/industry visits, investor pitch—gold for health product entrepreneurship.

opportunities.name: Hack The East
opportunities.what_you_get: 24-hour AI build; partner track awards; mentorship; internships/recognition from sponsors; demo + pitch.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: University students across Hong Kong (inter-university); competitive selection (~350 participants from ~650 registrants in 2026).
opportunities.apply_window: Jan–Feb for late Feb/early Mar weekend
opportunities.notes: Largest student-led AI hackathon vibe in HK; build-first culture (working demos valued).
opportunities.creative_angle: Ship a working AI product for education/health/finance with polished demo video and clear user value.
opportunities.why_great: Intense builder culture with product impact judging—great for prototyping speed and demo/pitch skills among peers.

opportunities.name: BUHack
opportunities.what_you_get: Beginner-friendly hybrid hack; theme tracks (e.g. digital wellbeing/productivity); design + demo emphasis; closing ceremony at HKBU.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Undergraduate students from all Hong Kong universities.
opportunities.apply_window: Feb for early Mar event (2026: 5–8 Mar)
opportunities.notes: First BUHack 2026 emphasised creativity, UX, and presentation (30% creativity / 20% design / 20% tech / 30% pitch).
opportunities.creative_angle: Win on UX story and wellbeing product narrative even with light tech.
opportunities.why_great: Judging weights design and pitch highly—perfect low-barrier practice for product design + storytelling.

opportunities.name: GDG On Campus Hackathon (HKUST)
opportunities.funder: Google Developer Groups On Campus (HKUST + sister HK chapters)
opportunities.what_you_get: Free pre-hack workshops (agentic AI, prompt eng, prototyping, pitching); curated ~100 spots; 1-day build + pitch; secondary + university tracks.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Secondary school and university students in HK; competitive application (~100 spots).
opportunities.notes: Explicit founder/innovator framing; workshops open to registered participants.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use workshops to level up, then pitch an AI agent product with crisp demo narrative.
opportunities.why_great: Built-in skill ramp (workshops) plus pitching focus makes it a strong starter for product + entrepreneurship confidence.

opportunities.name: CUHK Data Hack
opportunities.what_you_get: Pre-hack workshops; 48-hour interdisciplinary build on social-good data themes; e-voucher prizes; digital/data competency practice.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: CUHK students and staff (teams or individuals); check libguides for annual rules.
opportunities.apply_window: Early Mar (e.g. registration to 11 Mar 2026 for 13–15 Mar event)
opportunities.notes: 2026 theme: Data Solutions for Social Good (SDGs 11 & 12); venue University Library.
opportunities.creative_angle: Position as a civic product: data → insight → intervention prototype for a district need.
opportunities.why_great: Interdisciplinary 48-hour build with social-good framing—good for product sense using real data constraints.

opportunities.name: HKMU Hackathon
opportunities.funder: Hong Kong Metropolitan University Student Affairs Office
opportunities.what_you_get: Industry problem statements; company visits; BMC + pitching training; cash prizes; direct entry to MetroChallenge finals; partner internships; pathway to GBA contests.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: HKMU students (cross-discipline teams); verify if external students invited each year.
opportunities.apply_window: Nov–Dec for Jan event (2026 ended 8 Jan)
opportunities.notes: Emphasises creativity/problem-solving over pure coding; 2026 themes included smart property + pet health/wellness.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use BMC workshop to turn an industry pain into a testable service prototype.
opportunities.why_great: Explicit entrepreneurship training (BMC, pitch) tied to industry briefs and a follow-on funding competition.

opportunities.name: Cyberport University Partnership Programme (CUPP)
opportunities.what_you_get: Pre-camp digital tech training; overseas entrepreneurship boot camp (e.g. Draper University); mentorship; Demo Day; up to HK$100k CCMF grant for winners; CIP pathway.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Nomination-based via co-organising HK universities; typically age 18–30; enrolled students or recent grads; digital-tech projects.
opportunities.notes: Bootcamp + competition hybrid since 2015; FinTech/AI/blockchain/cybersecurity/data themes recent years.
opportunities.creative_angle: Package a digital health or fintech product with Silicon Valley bootcamp pitch narrative.
opportunities.why_great: Highest-signal HK student entrepreneurship programme: real grant, overseas immersive training, and investor Demo Day.

opportunities.name: Hong Kong Social Enterprise Challenge (HKSEC)
opportunities.funder: CUHK Centre for Entrepreneurship + Home and Youth Affairs Bureau
opportunities.what_you_get: Structured social-venture education + competition + incubation pathway; mentorship; prizes/seed support for youth-led social enterprises.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Post-secondary UG/PG students and fresh graduates from HK institutions (historically very broad institutional participation).
opportunities.notes: Running since 2007; more venture challenge than overnight hack—still core build/entrepreneurship skill building.
opportunities.creative_angle: Design a service product with measurable social outcomes and a viable revenue model.
opportunities.why_great: Longest-running HK youth social-venture pipeline—forces customer discovery, business modelling, and impact design together.

opportunities.name: JUMPSTARTER Global Pitch Competition
opportunities.what_you_get: Global startup pitch platform; mentoring/networking with corporates & investors; finals at HKCEC; investment opportunity up to US$5M for top startups; Alibaba ecosystem access.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Startups/founders globally (student teams possible if incorporated or venture-ready); competitive selection from hundreds of submissions.
opportunities.year_of_study: Founders / early startups (students welcome if venture-stage)
opportunities.apply_window: Typically autumn applications for spring finale
opportunities.notes: Not a weekend hackathon—flagship HK startup empowerment event; AI + sustainability themes recent years.
opportunities.creative_angle: Use after a local hackathon win: convert prototype into investment-ready pitch with traction plan.
opportunities.why_great: Best HK stage to practice high-stakes investor pitching and ecosystem networking once you have a real product.

opportunities.name: Community Shaper Hackathon
opportunities.funder: China Hong Kong Culture Exchange Association (ITC Innovation and Technology Fund)
opportunities.what_you_get: Design-thinking workshops; hybrid hackathon; forums across EduTech/HealthTech/FinTech/ClimateTech; certificates/reference letters; GBA youth I&T networking.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: Youth ~15–29 (edition-specific); teams of 3–5; programme registrants; open internationally with on-site preference.
opportunities.next_deadline_hint: Watch GlueUp / Devpost when next Community Shaper opens
opportunities.notes: ITC-funded talent programme with multiple forums + design-thinking hackathons; hybrid mainly on-site at Science Park historically.
opportunities.creative_angle: Treat forums as customer discovery, then ship a HealthTech/ClimateTech prototype in the hackathon sprint.
opportunities.why_great: End-to-end design-thinking journey across priority tech verticals—accessible skill building for product + entrepreneurship.

opportunities.name: Imperial Hackathon Hong Kong
opportunities.what_you_get: Policy-driven interdisciplinary challenges; mentoring; grand finale at HKSTP; prizes/special awards; STEMB + entrepreneurship experience; Imperial exposure.
opportunities.eligibility_summary: HK secondary students Grade 10+; teams of 4–5 from same school + adult supervisor; max 3 teams/school.
opportunities.notes: School-level but strong design/entrepreneurship pedagogy; free application.
opportunities.creative_angle: Pick wildcard/personal challenge and blend STEM + design + policy narrative.
opportunities.why_great: Interdisciplinary problem-to-pitch training with real HK policy themes—excellent early product entrepreneurship practice.
